finding: 48 S1 (denier) segments out of 4,902 classified. No scientist (M3) ever classified as S1.

indicator: False balance rate (S1/S2/S3 + S6 in same article)
finding: 121 of 1,189 articles with non-neutral stances show false balance. Concentrated in M6 politicians as the problematic voice.

indicator: M4 pseudoscientist share of all mentions
finding: Only 87 M4 mentions across 10 years. ČT does not platform alternative-science voices.
Crisis language is common (40–104 per 100 docs) but explicit action calls are rare (5–16 per 100 docs). Ratio declining over time — crisis without agency.
2,799 of 4,902 classified segments express no stance on climate action. Climate treated as information item, not as crisis requiring action.
Mitigation (18%) and adaptation (15%) topics roughly comparable. 2022 spike: geopolitics (Ukraine/energy) crowded out both frames.
EU Green Deal language grew 25× post-2020 — policy solutions are entering coverage. But driven by EU events, not ČT editorial initiative.
Citizens produce only 1,143 of 56,613 mentions. Coverage is dominated by élites (M6 politicians: 22.6%).
Climate justice, just transition, and global south framing virtually absent throughout the entire 10-year period.
Female speakers: 39.4% among journalists but only 10.7–12.6% among scientists, stakeholders, and politicians.
Low-income countries = 2.5%, lower-middle = 6.0% of all country mentions. 101 countries (inc. most climate-vulnerable) appear in <10 docs over 10 years.
